showFullCode.title: Show Complete File
showFullCode.description: Show complete file for code, with bootstrap and error handling included.

bitly_url.description: The unique bit.ly URL for this GIF

create_datetime.description: The date this GIF was added to the GIPHY database.

embded_url.description: A URL used for embedding this GIF

featured_tags.description: An array of featured tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

import_datetime.description: The creation or upload date from this GIF's source.

rating.description: The MPAA-style rating for this content. Examples include Y, G, PG, PG-13 and R

slug.description: The unique slug used in this GIF's URL

source.description: The page on which this GIF was found

source_post_url.description: The URL of the webpage on which this GIF was found.

source_tld.description: The top level domain of the source URL.

tags.description: An array of tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

trending_datetime.description: The date on which this gif was marked trending, if applicable.

update_datetime.description: The date on which this GIF was last updated.

url.description: The unique URL for this GIF

username.description: The username this GIF is attached to, if applicable

frames.description: The number of frames in this GIF.

height.description: The height of this GIF in pixels.

mp4.description: The URL for this GIF in .MP4 format.

mp4_size.description: The size in bytes of the .MP4 file corresponding to this GIF.

size.description: The size of this GIF in bytes.

url.description: The publicly-accessible direct URL for this GIF.

webp.description: The URL for this GIF in .webp format.

webp_size.description: The size in bytes of the .webp file corresponding to this GIF.

width.description: The width of this GIF in pixels.

Meta.title: Meta
Meta.description: The Meta Object contains basic information regarding the request, whether it was successful, and the response given by the API.  Check `responses` to see a description of types of response codes the API might give you under different cirumstances.

response_id.description: A unique ID paired with this response from the API.

Pagination.title: Pagination
Pagination.description: The Pagination Object contains information relating to the number of total results available as well as the number of results fetched and their relative positions.

User.title: User
User.description: The User Object contains information about the user associated with a GIF and URLs to assets such as that user's avatar image, profile, and more.

avatar_url.description: The URL for this user's avatar image.

banner_url.description: The URL for the banner image that appears atop this user's profile page.

display_name.description: The display name associated with this user (contains formatting the base username might not).

profile_url.description: The URL for this user's profile.

twitter.description: The Twitter username associated with this user, if applicable.

username.description: The username associated with this user.

Images.title: Images
Images.description: An object containing data for various available formats and sizes of this GIF.

Type.title: Type
Type.description: Type of the gif. By default, this is almost always gif

Nodes.Text: Giphy API
Official Giphy Documentation: [https://developers.giphy.com/docs/](https://developers.giphy.com/docs/)

The generated code has dependencies over external libraries like UniRest and JsonMapper. JsonMapper requires docblock annotations like `@var`, `@maps`, and `@factory` to map JSON responses with our class definitions. Hence the docblocks in generated code cannot be disabled by deactivating the PHP configurations like `opcache.save_comments`. These dependencies are defined in the `composer.json` file that comes with the SDK. To resolve these dependencies, we use the Composer package manager which requires PHP greater than or equal to 7.2 installed in your system. Visit [https://getcomposer.org/download/](https://getcomposer.org/download/) to download the installer file for Composer and run it in your system. Open command prompt and type `composer --version`. This should display the current version of the Composer installed if the installation was successful.

* Using command line, navigate to the directory containing the generated files (including `composer.json`) for the SDK. 
* Run the command `composer install`. This should install all the required dependencies and create the `vendor` directory in your project directory.

Nodes.Title: Configuring CURL Certificate Path in php.ini

Nodes.Text: **Note** This is for Windows users only.

CURL used to include a list of accepted CAs, but no longer bundles ANY CA certs. So by default it will reject all SSL certificates as unverifiable. You will have to get your CA's cert and point curl at it. The steps are as follows:

1. Download the certificate bundle (.pem file) from [https://curl.haxx.se/docs/caextract.html](https://curl.haxx.se/docs/caextract.html) on to your system.
2. Add curl.cainfo = "PATH_TO/cacert.pem" to your php.ini file located in your php installation. “PATH_TO” must be an absolute path containing the .pem file.

[curl]; A default value for the CURLOPT_CAINFO option. This is required to be an
; absolute path.
curl.cainfo = PATH_TO/cacert.pem

Nodes.SuggestedLinkText: Configuring CURL Certificate Path in php.ini

Nodes.Text: The following section explains how to use the GiphyApiLib library in a new project.
Open an IDE for PHP like PhpStorm. The basic workflow presented here is also applicable if you prefer using a different editor or IDE.
Click on `Open` in PhpStorm to browse to your generated SDK directory and then click `OK`.
Create a new directory by right clicking on the solution name as shown below:

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PHPStorm - Step 1

Nodes.Text: Name the directory as "test".

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PHPStorm - Step 2

Nodes.Text: Add a PHP file to this project.

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PHPStorm - Step 3

Nodes.Text: Name it "testSDK".

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PHPStorm - Step 4

Nodes.Text: Depending on your project setup, you might need to include composer's autoloader in your PHP code to enable auto loading of classes.
require_once "vendor/autoload.php";
It is important that the path inside require_once correctly points to the file `autoload.php` inside the vendor directory created during dependency installations.

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PHPStorm - Step 5

Nodes.Text: After this you can add code to initialize the client library and acquire the instance of a Api class. Sample code to initialize the client library and use the Api methods is given in the subsequent sections.
To run your project you must set the Interpreter for your project. Interpreter is the PHP engine installed on your computer.
Open `Settings` from `File` menu.
Select `PHP` from within `Languages & Frameworks`.
Browse for Interpreters near the `Interpreter` option and choose your interpreter.
Once the interpreter is selected, click `OK`.
To run your project, right click on your PHP file inside your Test project and click on `Run`.
The following parameters are configurable for the API Client:

Data:
Timeout for API calls in seconds.
*Default*: `30`

Whether to enable retries and backoff feature.
*Default*: `false`

The number of retries to make.
*Default*: `0`

The retry time interval between the endpoint calls.
*Default*: `1`

Exponential backoff factor to increase interval between retries.
*Default*: `2`

The maximum wait time in seconds for overall retrying requests.
*Default*: `0`

Whether to retry on request timeout.
*Default*: `true`
Represents the logging configurations for API calls
Represents the proxy configurations for API calls
The Credentials Setter for Custom Query Parameter

Nodes.Text: The API client can be initialized as follows:

This API uses the following authentication schemes.
* [`api_key (Custom Query Parameter)`]($h/__auth_CustomQueryAuthentication)
Documentation for accessing and setting credentials for api_key.

**Note:** Auth credentials can be set using `CustomQueryAuthenticationCredentialsBuilder::init()` in `customQueryAuthenticationCredentials` method in the client builder and accessed through `getCustomQueryAuthenticationCredentials` method in the client instance.
You must provide credentials in the client as shown in the following code snippet.

* [Gifs]($e/gifs)
* [Stickers]($e/stickers)
The singleton instance of the `GifsApi` class can be accessed from the API Client.
$gifsApi = $client->getGifsApi();

Nodes.Title: Get Gifs by Id
Nodes.Name: getGifsById
Nodes.Description: A multiget version of the get GIF by ID endpoint.
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `getResult()` method on this instance returns the response data which is of type [```GifsResponse```]($m/Gifs%20Response).

Parameters.Name: ids
Parameters.Description: Filters results by specified GIF IDs, separated by commas.

Errors.Name: ApiException
Errors.Description: Your request was formatted incorrectly or missing required parameters.

Errors.Name: ApiException
Errors.Description: You weren't authorized to make your request; most likely this indicates an issue with your API Key.

Errors.Name: ApiException
Errors.Description: The particular GIF you are requesting was not found. This occurs, for example, if you request a GIF by an id that does not exist.

Errors.Name: ApiException
Errors.Description: Your API Key is making too many requests. Read about [requesting a Production Key](https://developers.giphy.com/docs/#access) to upgrade your API Key rate limits.

ids.description: Filters results by specified GIF IDs, separated by commas.

Nodes.Title: Search Gifs
Nodes.Name: searchGifs
Nodes.Description: Search all GIPHY GIFs for a word or phrase. Punctuation will be stripped and ignored.  Use a plus or url encode for phrases. Example paul+rudd, ryan+gosling or american+psycho.
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `getResult()` method on this instance returns the response data which is of type [```GifsSearchResponse```]($m/Gifs%20Search%20Response).

MethodSignature.Text:
function searchGifs(
    string $q,
    ?int $limit = 25,
    ?int $offset = 0,
    ?string $rating = null,
    ?string $lang = null
): ApiResponse

Parameters.Name: limit
Parameters.Description:
The maximum number of records to return.

**Default**: `25`

Parameters.Name: lang
Parameters.Description: Specify default language for regional content; use a 2-letter ISO 639-1 language code.

Errors.Name: ApiException
Errors.Description: Your request was formatted incorrectly or missing required parameters.

Errors.Name: ApiException

lang.description: Specify default language for regional content; use a 2-letter ISO 639-1 language code.

Nodes.Title: Search Stickers
Nodes.Name: searchStickers
Nodes.Description: Replicates the functionality and requirements of the classic GIPHY search, but returns animated stickers rather than GIFs.
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `getResult()` method on this instance returns the response data which is of type [```StickersSearchResponse```]($m/Stickers%20Search%20Response).

MethodSignature.Text:
function searchStickers(
    string $q,
    ?int $limit = 25,
    ?int $offset = 0,
    ?string $rating = null,
    ?string $lang = null
): ApiResponse

Parameters.Name: limit
Parameters.Description:
The maximum number of records to return.

**Default**: `25`

Parameters.Name: lang
Parameters.Description: Specify default language for regional content; use a 2-letter ISO 639-1 language code.

Fields.Name: bitlyUrl
Fields.Description: The unique bit.ly URL for this GIF

Fields.Name: contentUrl
Fields.Description: Currently unused

Fields.Name: createDatetime
Fields.Description: The date this GIF was added to the GIPHY database.

Fields.Name: embdedUrl
Fields.Description: A URL used for embedding this GIF

Fields.Name: featuredTags
Fields.Description: An array of featured tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

Fields.Name: id
Fields.Description: This GIF's unique ID

Fields.Name: images
Fields.Description: An object containing data for various available formats and sizes of this GIF.

Fields.Name: importDatetime
Fields.Description: The creation or upload date from this GIF's source.

Fields.Name: rating
Fields.Description: The MPAA-style rating for this content. Examples include Y, G, PG, PG-13 and R

Fields.Name: slug
Fields.Description: The unique slug used in this GIF's URL

Fields.Name: source
Fields.Description: The page on which this GIF was found

Fields.Name: sourcePostUrl
Fields.Description: The URL of the webpage on which this GIF was found.

Fields.Name: sourceTld
Fields.Description: The top level domain of the source URL.

Fields.Name: tags
Fields.Description: An array of tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

Fields.Name: trendingDatetime
Fields.Description: The date on which this gif was marked trending, if applicable.

Fields.Name: type
Fields.Description:
Type of the gif. By default, this is almost always gif

**Default**: `Type::GIF`

Fields.Name: updateDatetime
Fields.Description: The date on which this GIF was last updated.

Fields.Name: url
Fields.Description: The unique URL for this GIF

Fields.Name: user
Fields.Description: The User Object contains information about the user associated with a GIF and URLs to assets such as that user's avatar image, profile, and more.

Fields.Name: username
Fields.Description: The username this GIF is attached to, if applicable

Nodes.Title: Gif
Nodes.Name: Gif
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type array.*

Example.Text:
use GiphyApiLib\Models\Builders\GifBuilder;
use GiphyApiLib\Utils\DateTimeHelper;
use GiphyApiLib\ApiHelper;
use GiphyApiLib\Models\Type;

$gif = GifBuilder::init()
    ->bitlyUrl('http://gph.is/1gsWDcL')
    ->contentUrl('content_url4')
    ->createDatetime(DateTimeHelper::fromRfc3339DateTime('2013-08-01 12:41:48'))
    ->embdedUrl('http://giphy.com/embed/YsTs5ltWtEhnq')
    ->featuredTags(
        [
            'featured_tags0'
        ]
    )
    ->id('YsTs5ltWtEhnq')
    ->importDatetime(DateTimeHelper::fromRfc3339DateTime('2013-08-01 12:41:48'))
    ->rating('g')
    ->slug('confused-flying-YsTs5ltWtEhnq')
    ->source('http://www.reddit.com/r/reactiongifs/comments/1xpyaa/superman_goes_to_hollywood/')
    ->sourcePostUrl('http://cheezburger.com/5282328320')
    ->sourceTld('cheezburger.com')
    ->trendingDatetime(DateTimeHelper::fromRfc3339DateTime('2013-08-01 12:41:48'))
    ->type(Type::GIF)
    ->updateDatetime(DateTimeHelper::fromRfc3339DateTime('2013-08-01 12:41:48'))
    ->url('http://giphy.com/gifs/confused-flying-YsTs5ltWtEhnq')
    ->username('JoeCool4000')
    ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
    ->build();

Fields.Name: frames
Fields.Description: The number of frames in this GIF.

Fields.Name: height
Fields.Description: The height of this GIF in pixels.

Fields.Name: mp4
Fields.Description: The URL for this GIF in .MP4 format.

Fields.Name: mp4Size
Fields.Description: The size in bytes of the .MP4 file corresponding to this GIF.

Fields.Name: size
Fields.Description: The size of this GIF in bytes.

Fields.Name: url
Fields.Description: The publicly-accessible direct URL for this GIF.

Fields.Name: webp
Fields.Description: The URL for this GIF in .webp format.

Fields.Name: webpSize
Fields.Description: The size in bytes of the .webp file corresponding to this GIF.

Fields.Name: width
Fields.Description: The width of this GIF in pixels.

Fields.Name: username

                ->bitlyUrl('bitly_url0')
                ->contentUrl('content_url4')
                ->createDatetime(DateTimeHelper::fromRfc3339DateTime('2016-03-13T12:52:32.123Z'))
                ->embdedUrl('embded_url8')
                ->featuredTags(
                    [
                        'featured_tags0'
                    ]
                )
                ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
                ->build(),
            GifBuilder::init()
                ->bitlyUrl('bitly_url0')
                ->contentUrl('content_url4')
                ->createDatetime(DateTimeHelper::fromRfc3339DateTime('2016-03-13T12:52:32.123Z'))
                ->embdedUrl('embded_url8')
                ->featuredTags(
                    [
                        'featured_tags0'
                    ]
                )
                ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
                ->build(),
            GifBuilder::init()
                ->bitlyUrl('bitly_url0')
                ->contentUrl('content_url4')
                ->createDatetime(DateTimeHelper::fromRfc3339DateTime('2016-03-13T12:52:32.123Z'))
                ->embdedUrl('embded_url8')
                ->featuredTags(
                    [
                        'featured_tags0'
                    ]
                )
                ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
                ->build()
        ]
    )
    ->meta(
        MetaBuilder::init()
            ->msg('msg8')
            ->responseId('response_id0')
            ->status(98)
            ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
            ->build()
    )
    ->pagination(
        PaginationBuilder::init()
            ->count(192)
            ->offset(240)
            ->totalCount(100)
            ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
            ->build()
    )
    ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
    ->build();

Example.Text:
use GiphyApiLib\Models\Builders\ImagesBuilder;

$images = ImagesBuilder::init()
    ->downsized(

            ->frames('frames0')

            ->mp4Size('mp4_size2')
            ->size('size2')

    ->downsizedLarge(
        ImageBuilder::init()
            ->frames('frames6')
            ->height('height4')

            ->mp4Size('mp4_size8')
            ->size('size8')

            ->build()
    )
    ->downsizedMedium(
        ImageBuilder::init()
            ->frames('frames2')
            ->height('height0')
            ->mp4('mp42')
            ->mp4Size('mp4_size4')
            ->size('size4')
            ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
            ->build()
    )
    ->downsizedSmall(
        ImageBuilder::init()
            ->frames('frames0')
            ->height('height8')

            ->mp4Size('mp4_size2')
            ->size('size2')
            ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
            ->build()
    )
    ->downsizedStill(
        ImageBuilder::init()
            ->frames('frames4')
            ->height('height4')
            ->mp4('mp46')
            ->mp4Size('mp4_size8')
            ->size('size2')
            ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
            ->build()
    )
    ->additionalProperty('exampleAdditionalProperty', ApiHelper::deserialize('{"key1":"val1","key2":"val2"}'))
    ->build();

Nodes.Text: Represents the proxy server configurations for API calls. Create instance using `ProxyConfigurationBuilder::init('http://your.proxy.host')`
Methods

Data: Sets the proxy host address. Should be set during initialization via init() method.
Sets the port used to connect to the proxy server. **Default port:** 0
Enables or disables tunneling through the proxy server. **Default tunnel:** false
Sets both username and password in a single method. **Default user:** '', **Default pass:** ''
Sets the proxy authentication method. **Default authMethod:** CURLAUTH_BASIC

Nodes.Text: Client Initialization with Proxy Configuration
To configure the SDK to use a proxy server, initialize the proxy configuration during client setup as shown in the Usage Example.

CodeExamples.Title: Usage Example
CodeExamples.Text:
<?php

use GiphyApiLib\GiphyApiClientBuilder;
use GiphyApiLib\Proxy\ProxyConfigurationBuilder;
// initialize the sdk client using a proxy configuration
$client = GiphyApiClientBuilder::init()
    ->proxyConfiguration(
        ProxyConfigurationBuilder::init('http://localhost')
            ->port(8080)
            ->auth('username', 'password')
            ->authMethod(CURLAUTH_BASIC)
            ->tunnel(true)
    )
    ->build();

Nodes.Text: Represents the logging configurations for API calls. Create instance using `LoggingConfigurationBuilder::init()`
Methods

Data: Takes in your custom implementation of the Psr\Log\LoggerInterface.php. **Default Implementation : `ConsoleLogger`**
Defines the log message severity mentioned in Psr\Log\LogLevel.php (e.g., DEBUG, INFO, etc). **Default : `logLevel::INFO`**
Toggles the global setting to mask sensitive HTTP headers in both requests and responses before logging, safeguarding confidential data. **Default : `true`**
The logging configurations for an API request.
The logging configurations for an API response.

Nodes.Text: Usage Example
Client Initialization with Custom Logger

In order to provide custom logger, any implementation of the `Psr\Log\LoggerInterface` can be used so that you can override the `log` behavior and provide its instance directly in the SDK client initialization.
The following example uses `Monolog\Logger` implementation of `Psr\Log\LoggerInterface` for GiphyApiClient initialization.

<?php

use GiphyApiLib\GiphyApiClientBuilder;
use GiphyApiLib\Logging\LoggingConfigurationBuilder;
use GiphyApiLib\Logging\RequestLoggingConfigurationBuilder;
use GiphyApiLib\Logging\ResponseLoggingConfigurationBuilder;
use Psr\Log\LogLevel;
use Monolog\Logger;
use Monolog\Handler\StreamHandler;

// create a log channel
$logger = new Logger('GiphyApi');
$logger->pushHandler(new StreamHandler(__DIR__ . '/api_data.log'));

// initialize the sdk client using this logger
$client = GiphyApiClientBuilder::init()
    ->loggingConfiguration(
        LoggingConfigurationBuilder::init()
            ->logger($logger)
            ->level(LogLevel::INFO)
            ->requestConfiguration(RequestLoggingConfigurationBuilder::init()->body(true))
            ->responseConfiguration(ResponseLoggingConfigurationBuilder::init()->headers(true))
    )
    ->build();
Represents the logging configurations for API requests. Create instance using `RequestLoggingConfigurationBuilder::init()`
Methods

Data: Toggles the inclusion of query parameters in the logged request path. **Default : `false`**
Toggles the logging of the request body. **Default : `false`**
Toggles the logging of the request headers. **Default : `false`**
Includes only specified request headers in the log output. **Default : `[]`**
Excludes specified request headers from the log output. **Default : `[]`**
Logs specified request headers without masking, revealing their actual values. **Default : `[]`**

Nodes.Text: Represents the logging configurations for API responses. Create instance using `ResponseLoggingConfigurationBuilder::init()`
Methods

Data: Toggles the logging of the request body. **Default : `false`**
Toggles the logging of the request headers. **Default : `false`**
Includes only specified request headers in the log output. **Default : `[]`**
Excludes specified request headers from the log output. **Default : `[]`**
Logs specified request headers without masking, revealing their actual values. **Default : `[]`**

Nodes.Text: Represents a single Http Request.
Methods

Data: The HTTP method of the request.
The endpoint URL for the API request.

Nodes.Text: Represents the result of an API call, including the request details, response metadata, and the returned data.
Methods

Data: Returns the original request that resulted in this response.
Returns the original body from the response.
Checks if the response is successful (HTTP 2xx).
Checks if the response indicates an error. (not HTTP 2xx)

Nodes.Text: This developer portal is powered by **APIMatic**. The same platform that generated this experience can turn any OpenAPI specification into a complete developer portal — interactive documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, live API testing, and AI-assisted onboarding — in minutes.

Context Plugins load your full API context — docs, SDKs, code samples, and setup instructions — into AI coding environments like Claude Code and Cursor, so developers integrate your API through natural language instead of a wall of reference docs. A built-in portal copilot is available too.

API Reference documentation with language‑specific docs and SDK guides — including idiomatic, ready-to-run code samples complete with imports across all seven SDK languages. Developers can explore your API structure before writing any code.
Built-in testing interface supporting all major authentication methods. Developers can make real API calls, see actual responses, and validate their integration approach.
Production-ready client libraries in Java, Python, TypeScript, C#, PHP, Ruby, and Go. Each SDK includes comprehensive documentation, code samples, and follows language-specific best practices.
Step-by-step onboarding flows to help developers implement key API usecases faster.
Full control over colors, fonts, logos, and layout. Add custom documentation pages, modify navigation, and ensure the portal reflects your brand identity.
Bring your API to APIMatic and ship a developer portal like this one in minutes.
See how APIMatic turns your API specification into a branded, AI-ready developer portal.
Dive into the Advanced Features Guide to set up and customize your own portal.

Nodes.Title: How quickly can we launch our Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: You can generate and publish your Developer Portal in minutes. Simply upload your OpenAPI specification, customize the look and feel to match your brand, and publish instantly - either as a standalone site or embedded into your existing Developer Portal.

Nodes.Title: How does the Portal stay current as our API evolves?

Nodes.Text: APIMatic automatically updates your portal when your API specification changes. You can re-import updated API definitions, and the portal will regenerate with the latest endpoints, parameters, and documentation while preserving your customizations.

Nodes.Title: What level of customization and control do we have?

Nodes.Text: You can completely customize the look, feel and behavior of your Developer Experience Portal, including primary colors, themes, custom sections, navigation links, custom URL slugs, and branding. You can also choose to host it on your own custom domain or embed it seamlessly into your existing documentation site using a simple HTML script.

Nodes.Title: Is there a way we can integrate this with our existing Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: Absolutely. APIMatic provides embeddable portal components that can be seamlessly integrated into your existing documentation sites using a simple HTML script. You can also choose to host the portal separately on your own domain while maintaining your existing portal structure.

Nodes.Title: What AI capabilities does this Developer Portal have?

Nodes.Text: **Context Plugins** let developers connect your API to the AI coding assistant they already use (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, and more) to search, ask questions, and generate working integration code grounded in your live specification.

The portal also includes a built-in **API Copilot** that answers questions from your API reference and product documentation, and an **AI Assist Menu** that sends any documentation page straight to your preferred AI tool as context with one click.
Automatic **LLMs.txt generation** and per-page Markdown exports make your documentation AI-accessible to tools like Cursor, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Claude.
